note: Generated by scripts/roadmap-to-json.js. Do not edit by hand; edit ROADMAP.md and regenerate.

now.text: Documentation refactor. Audience-first structure (separate clear paths for users, admins, AI, and contributors), an em-dash-free content sweep, and this roadmap becoming the one canonical to-do list. In progress this session.

Next gameplay arc (operator's pick): either the garden plot-types registry (soil / sand / pots / trays / direct-sow as data-driven growing methods) or the First Playable arc (walk-up crafting stations, a 3D heads-up vitals display, death and respawn, a guided first day).
Security sprint tail. Sign each published release; ship the member-directory opt-out toggle; enable GitHub branch protection.

themes.title: Survival and self-sufficiency
themes.description: The heart of the mission: in-game systems that map to real-world systems a person can

items.text: Crafting loop, mine to refine to craft, with a 2-tier ore to ingot to alloy chain.
Cooking, nutrition, and vitals: eat, decay, conditions, well-fed.
Gardening: plant, water, harvest, with a Garden panel.
Drone and asteroid mining: commission, trip, mine a finite asteroid, deliver.
Skills and XP, with tech-unlock gating and a quest chain.
Aeroponic towers end to end: curated configs, a compatibility check, a 3D placeholder, the inventory tree, plant to grow to harvest, and a real-world parts list.
Seed economy: survival mode consumes seeds, a starter grant, and a harvest that returns seeds so the loop sustains itself.
Homestead designs: the Fibonacci self-sufficient blueprint as a browsable design, plus a self-sufficiency model (coupled energy / water / food / waste loops).
Save and load: offline progress (inventory and skills) now persists between sessions.
Garden plot-types registry: generalize "tower" into a data-driven growing method (aeroponic / soil / sand / pot / raised-bed / direct-sow / trays), each moddable, none hardcoded.
First Playable arc: persistence depth, a 3D vitals HUD, walk-up stations, death and respawn, and a guided first day.

Real-hardware control layer: bind a home to real monitoring and automation hardware (for example real aeroponics, solar, water), the north star where the game becomes the control panel for your actual homestead.
Parts-list-to-real: for every buildable system, a refined bill of materials you can 3D-print, buy, or trade for.

themes.title: Identity and privacy
themes.description: You own your identity and your data. No accounts, no passwords, no tracking. Even the

items.text: Post-quantum identity: a Dilithium3 / ML-DSA-65 key derived from a BIP39 24-word seed (which is also your Solana wallet). The canonical crypto details live in the Cryptography section of CLAUDE.md.
End-to-end encrypted DMs: pure Kyber768 / ML-KEM-768 to BLAKE3-KDF to AES-256-GCM. The relay stores only ciphertext.
Encrypted vault (PBKDF2 600k both clients) with three auto-unlock modes.
BIP39 24-word recovery and social recovery, so losing a device never locks you out forever.
Proof-of-possession at connect: a signed challenge-response before the relay binds your identity (closes identity spoofing).
Release signing: a hybrid Ed25519 + Dilithium3 signature on every release (both must verify), so a compromised GitHub or a stray tag can never push code to your machine.
Member-directory opt-out: a privacy setting so you can join a server without appearing in its public member list, with a user-facing toggle.
Multi-device key sync: authorize a second device by QR or short code over an encrypted channel (part of the device mesh).
STARK selective disclosure: prove a fact about yourself without revealing the underlying data.

themes.title: Communication and federation
themes.description: Talk, meet, and organize without a gatekeeper. Servers are meeting places, not owners

items.text: Chat: channels, threaded replies, reactions, pins, search, and direct messages.
Voice and video calling on the web (WebRTC peer-to-peer with screen share).
Peer-to-peer groups: signed objects, end-to-end encryption, and a group that behaves like a channel, on both web and native (Phases 1 and 2).
Signed profile replication: your profile gossips between servers, so there is no single home server to lose.
Web-to-native parity: rebuilding the web chat view to mirror the native app one-to-one (Track W).
Federation activation: an admin UI to add and trust peer servers, a second operator-run relay, end-to-end federation testing, then vetted third-party peers.
Peer-to-peer groups Phases 3 to 5: relay-independent transport (a group survives even if its home relay dies) and serverless discovery.
Native voice and streaming: the WebRTC transport that native currently lacks (web users have voice today; native users are observer-only).
Calendar with RSVP: events, recurring schedules, group calendars, reminders.
Mobile clients for Android and iOS.

themes.title: Governance and economy
themes.description: Turn groups into cooperatives. Coordinate work, trade fairly, and govern by a

items.text: The Humanity Accord: a CC0 constitution that binds the platform, readable in-app.
Tasks and mission control: a Fibonacci-scoped board for coordinating work at every scale.
Marketplace: listings, images, reviews, and seller ratings.
Reputation: peer-endorsed skills signed with your identity key.
Group governance: proposals, ranked-choice voting, and quorum rules, with every vote signed and tamper-evident.
Native trade UI completion (the page exists; the trade events need wiring).
Learning paths: complete a module, get peer-endorsed, unlock the next.
AI agent governance: evolve Article 14 of the Accord from documented intent into enforced rules with appeals as more AI participants join.

themes.title: The simulation
themes.description: A 3D educational world where you learn real survival and production skills by doing.

items.text: Engine: a wgpu PBR renderer, an ECS, and 40-plus game systems wired in.
World: icosphere planets with level-of-detail, voxel asteroids, and a ship-at-origin starting world.
The full production, survival, and progression sandbox (the gameplay-loop arc, v0.329 to v0.342).
UI consolidation: a slim top navigation, universal reusable widgets, the Humanity Mission Dashboard, and the in-app Library.
First Playable: walk-up stations, a 3D vitals HUD, and a guided first day (shared with the survival theme above).
Game-world depth: more content, characters, and deeper coupled systems.
Real-terrain world generation from real elevation data (USGS / SRTM / Copernicus) keyed to a place's latitude and longitude.
VR support and a "boot straight into Play" mode.

themes.title: Accessibility and onboarding
themes.description: The mission requires that anyone, of any age or background or ability, can use this.

items.text: Accessibility modes: high-contrast, colorblind, and reduced-motion.
Glossary overlay on the web (150-plus plain-language term definitions).
Localization in 5 languages (English, Spanish, French, Japanese, Chinese).
The Mission Dashboard: makes "what I am doing" and "what we are doing together" instantly clear to anyone.
Three-audience onboarding: clear, separate getting-started guides for standard users, server admins, and AI agents (this refactor).
A tooltip on every interactive element, in plain language.
A "first 5 minutes" guided tour for brand-new users.
Localization expansion: Arabic, Hindi, Portuguese, Russian, German, Swahili, and more.
A native glossary widget to match the web.
A full WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit with fixes.

themes.title: Infrastructure and sovereignty
themes.description: The platform must survive disasters, censorship, and the maintainer stepping away.

items.text: One unified binary: full desktop app, or headless relay server, from the same code.
Resilience: off-site 3-2-1 backups, SQLite corruption recovery, a liveness watchdog with self-heal, and multi-channel alerting.
Abuse resistance: fail2ban, per-key rate limits, signed-request anti-replay, and per-author submission quotas (the security sprint, v0.420 to v0.422).
Distribution: GitHub plus a self-hosted Forgejo mirror plus a BitTorrent seeder.
In-app ops console: bring every admin action into the app (the GUI-first mandate), paying down the CLI-only debt.
Device mesh: a "My Devices" dashboard, backup designation, restore flow, and direct LAN sync, so your own devices back each other up.
Litestream continuous replication (roughly 1-minute recovery point).
GitHub branch protection and required signed commits on main.
LoRa mesh radio integration (needs hardware on hand).
Distribution beyond GitHub: Codeberg, Software Heritage, a WinGet manifest, and IPFS.

recent.text: Vault-sync anti-replay; release signing activated and independently verified.
Release signing ACTIVATED: hybrid Ed25519 + Dilithium3, fail-closed updater.
Security sprint: relay rate limits, per-author object quotas, gossip-flood caps.
Signed-manifest auto-updater and signed local-build launcher.
The relay-build fix (CI had been red for 25 releases) plus retired-page cleanup.
The universal nested-expandable widget, inline item cards, and Mining and places on universal rows.
Homestead designs and the first working save and load.
The gameplay-loop arc: the full survival, production, and progression sandbox.
